sentences: You wish to know all about my grandfather. Well, he is nearly ninety-three years old; he dresses himself in the ancient Italian fashion each morning with some help from his son-in-law. When he speaks, his voice trembles a bit, and it has a high, thin, piping quality. He has a long, white, flowing beard, and it is a source of pride to him.
Do you know the story of the very hungry caterpillar? He ate through one apple, two pears, three plums, four strawberries, and five oranges, but he was still hungry.

Picture Description.instruction: Look at the image provided by the clinician and describe what is happening.

Word & Phrasal Stress.instruction: Please read these five sentences naturally:
sentences: The supermarket chain shut down because of poor management.
Much more money must be donated to make this department succeed.
In this famous coffee shop they serve the best doughnuts in town.
The chairman decided to pave over the shopping center garden.
The standards committee met this afternoon in an open meeting.

Word & Phrasal Stress.instruction: Answer the clinician's questions using the phrase: 'She hid his key'
sentences: Target Phrase: She hid his key
A strong job interview actually breathes life into my morning.
The other difficult colleague will perhaps confront my behavior and enjoy it.
Tell me the days of the week.

Resonance.instruction: Repeat these phrases while the clinician checks your breathing.

Diadochokinetic Rate (DDK).instruction: Repeat the sound as fast and steadily as you can until told to stop.
Repeat these sounds in a sequence as fast as you can.

Voice & Respiration.instruction: Take a deep breath and hold out the sound 'ah' for as long as you can.

sentences: Bite down five times, like you're chewing gum.
Smile and hold for three seconds.
Puff your cheeks up with air.
Stick your tongue straight out. Hold for three seconds.
Curl your tongue up towards your nose, then down towards your chin.
Push your tongue into the inside of your cheek.
